Evidence: 17 of 30 programs passed all hidden test cases.
Educational Interpretation: Students should verify AI-generated code through systematic testing before trusting the output.

Evidence: 4 benchmark problems showed fully consistent behaviour across all three generations.
Educational Interpretation: Repeated generations of the same prompt may not always produce equally reliable solutions.
Program execution alone should not be considered evidence of correctness.
Students should learn to identify syntax and execution issues before relying on AI-generated programs.
